stages.objective: Observe timer-driven preemption events in CPU trace.
stages.goal: Confirm proc.preempt appears with follow-up dispatch behavior.
stages.theory_detail: Preemption protects responsiveness by forcing periodic release of CPU ownership.

hints.concept: Preemption differs from blocking; it is scheduler/timer-driven.
hints.explicit: Run until multiple preempt-dispatch pairs appear in trace.

stages.objective: Diagnose whether preemption still yields full workload completion.
stages.goal: Pass preemption evidence and completion requirement together.

hints.concept: Correct preemption should preserve eventual completion.
hints.explicit: Continue execution until both jobs exit, then submit.

stages.objective: Tune scheduling and verify preemption correctness is preserved.
stages.goal: Apply policy adjustments while keeping preemption and completion checks passing.

hints.concept: Tuning changes cadence, not the need for valid preempt/dispatch flow.
hints.explicit: Apply one change, rerun, and verify proc.preempt plus completion checks.
